title: Exploring the roles of ribosomal peptides in prokaryote-phage interactions through deep learning-enabled metagenome mining
research_objectives: Explore biosynthetic profile of RiPPs in ocean microbiome
Investigate RiPP roles in prokaryote-phage interactions
Develop deep learning method for RiPP detection in fragmented metagenomes
Assess ecological functions of RiPPs in ocean

methodology.scope: Ribosomally synthesized and post-translationally modified peptides (RiPPs)

tripp_deep_learning_model.name: TrRiPP
tripp_deep_learning_model.advantage: Detects RiPPs independent of intact PTM enzymes
tripp_deep_learning_model.benefit: Applicable to highly fragmented metagenomic data

data_sources.diversity: Vast biomes rich in unique interactions
data_sources.scope: Ocean microbiota representing largest biological virus-microbe ecosystem

ecological_context.significance: Dynamic prokaryote-phage battles in ocean environment
ecological_context.research_opportunity: Investigate RiPP roles in microbe-virus interactions

traditional_limitations: antiSMASH relies on intact PTM enzyme detection
Misses RiPP families without classic enzyme signatures
